findings.reasoning: I found no reachable packed-field alias. Generic packed writes clear only `(mask << shift)` and OR the masked value (`contracts/libraries/BitPackingLib.sol:94-111`); prize-pool half writers preserve the sibling half (`contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:845-861`); balance half debits guard against low-half borrow/high-half truncation (`contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:922-990`); LR field writes are masked RMW (`contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:1568-1586`). The EV-cap two-window helper updates only a stamped 88-bit window (`contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:1713-1754`), and callers key it consistently at the resolver live level: mint buy `cachedLevel + 1` (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:1755-1802`), afking cover `currentLevel + 1` (`contracts/modules/GameAfkingModule.sol:970-1045`), and opens/redemptions `level + 1` (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:547-573`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:969-981`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:1089-1101`). Ticket keys use disjoint namespace bits (`contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:771-787`). DEC terminal isolation is explicit: terminal writes and reads `decBucketOffsetPacked[lvl + 1]`, with the regular future round precluded by gameover (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDecimatorModule.sol:1017-1024`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDecimatorModule.sol:1066-1100`). Slot-5 reverseFlip is a typed uint64 write preserving the co-resident timestamp (`contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:1817-1827`; layout at `contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:395-405`).

I found no exploitable write-after-write window. Mint records payment and mint data before returning to the purchase flow (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:2097-2103`), then lootbox day/streak/curse/score helpers run before the cached score is consumed (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:1577-1580`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:1695-1710`). Degenerette batched resolves use an in-memory running pool and flush once (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDegeneretteModule.sol:401-459`); regular recirculated boxes disable ETH-spin RMW (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol:889-910`), while box-origin ETH spin flushes claimable/pool before recirculation (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDegeneretteModule.sol:1435-1462`). Advance consolidation computes in memory and commits pools/claimable in one terminal batch (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:910-1003`). External calls on these paths are to pinned protocol contracts/self-dispatch, not player-controlled callbacks.

Normal delegatecall failures bubble and revert the whole frame, e.g. game stubs revert delegate failure data (`contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:1043-1047`, `contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:1073-1079`). The deliberately swallowed gameover ticket drain is not a partial-commit path: if `processTicketBatch` reverts, its delegatecall writes revert and the code falls through to `handleGameOverDrain`; if it succeeds but is unfinished, the function returns before terminal drain (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:647-689`). The terminal drain itself hard-reverts on failure and only then unlocks RNG (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:691-699`). sDGNRS redemption claims delete/reduce pending state before outbound value, but a later revert reverts the whole transaction (`contracts/sDGNRS.sol:865-929`).

I found no player-controlled reentrancy that can observe or exploit half-updated accounting. `claimWinnings` debits both packed halves and `claimablePool` before payout (`contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:1229-1254`), then `maybeCurse` is a delegatecall-only storage update with no external call (`contracts/modules/GameAfkingModule.sol:1744-1772`). The stETH fallback fix is present: stETH is transferred first and the untrusted ETH `.call` is last, after ledger debits (`contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:1888-1916`); sDGNRS uses the same order in `_payEth` (`contracts/sDGNRS.sol:1147-1170`). `subscribe` settles pending FLIP before `AFFILIATE.claim` (`contracts/modules/GameAfkingModule.sol:364-381`), `_settlePendingFlip` zeroes before `coinflip.creditFlip` (`contracts/modules/GameAfkingModule.sol:1085-1109`), and affiliate base drains read-and-zero under Affiliate-only access (`contracts/modules/GameAfkingModule.sol:1708-1721`).

findings.trigger: Trigger a regular Decimator settlement with nonzero winning burns: reach `advanceGame` consolidation at an eligible Decimator level, have `runDecimatorJackpot(decPoolWei,lvl,rngWord)` snapshot winners and return 0, then observe `claimablePool` increased by `spend` while individual winner balances are not yet credited. The same reserve pattern exists for terminal Decimator at gameover.
findings.reasoning: The literal identity `claimablePool == sum(balancesPacked claimable+afking)` is false during reachable Decimator reserve windows. The storage file explicitly documents this exception (`contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:358-365`). In consolidation, Decimator spend is removed from future pool and added to `claimableDelta`, then committed to `claimablePool` (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:937-952`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:996-1003`). `runDecimatorJackpot` stores `round.poolWei/totalBurn` and returns 0 without crediting player balances (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDecimatorModule.sol:263-279`). Later claims credit winners against that prior reserve; the lootbox half decreases `claimablePool` instead of adding it (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDecimatorModule.sol:398-415`, `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDecimatorModule.sol:446-463`). Terminal gameover does the same reserve add for terminal Decimator (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:174-181`) and later terminal claims credit without a new pool add (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameDecimatorModule.sol:1038-1046`). This is not an unbacked-credit or half-write corruption by itself; it means the correct liability identity must include outstanding Decimator claim rounds, not only per-player packed balances. A second intentional terminal exception exists after final sweep: non-sink player balances may remain in storage while `claimablePool` is zeroed and claims are disabled (`contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol:195-217`, `contracts/DegenerusGame.sol:1229-1231`).

overallVerdict: No catastrophic packed-storage aliasing, mid-advance reentrancy corruption, or unbacked sDGNRS redemption path found. The only reachable counterexample is CORRUPT-05 as written: `claimablePool` is sometimes a reserve superset, not exactly the sum of live per-player packed balances.
